DivisionImportExport.metaTitle: Import & Export Paraguay · Mercosur | Phoenix Global Holding
DivisionImportExport.metaDescription: International trade from Paraguay: NCM, Common External Tariff, Mercosur integration, customs compliance and regional logistics.
DivisionImportExport.title: Phoenix Global Import and Export
DivisionImportExport.subtitle: International trade infrastructure with governance, correct tariff classification and regional integration.
DivisionImportExport.intro: We structure high value-added imports, exports, South American redistribution and direct sales channels — with a focus on documentary compliance, traceability and logistics efficiency. Each line is run with audit-grade criteria and alignment to the prevailing regulatory framework.
DivisionImportExport.disclaimer: Institutional information only. It does not replace legal, tax or customs advice for specific operations, NCM positions or special regimes.
DivisionImportExport.pillarsLead: Transparency in classification, documentary consistency and alignment with the bloc and Paraguayan national law.

pillars.title: Nomenclature and taxation
pillars.body: Disciplined use of the NCM (Mercosur Common Nomenclature), aligned with the Harmonized System (HS), as the basis for customs and statistical treatment — with review when the good or its use changes classification.

pillars.title: Common External Tariff (CET)
pillars.body: Reference CET for non-bloc origins under Mercosur treaties and GMC/CMC decisions. National law maps the current tariff with local requirements; rates and benefits evolve — commercial plans must track the instruments in force.

pillars.title: Clearance and competent authorities
pillars.body: In Paraguay, the National Customs Directorate (DNA) leads customs control. Exporters and importers must follow declarations, channels and regimes suited to each operation, including origin documentation where applicable.

pillars.title: Regional integration
pillars.body: Intra-Mercosur and third-country flows involve different rules of origin, benefits and logistics. We model routes and partners to reduce friction at borders and in warehousing, always respecting the framework for each corridor.

pillars.title: Technology and data
pillars.body: When volume requires it, we integrate systems, tracking and automation with Phoenix Global Developer — without diluting compliance controls.

pillars.title: Corporate scalability
pillars.body: Larger operations may align with vehicles and governance via Enterprise Solution, keeping trade, technology and the holding clearly separated.

DivisionImportExport.frameworkLead: Mercosur organises trade among State Parties from the Treaty of Asunción (1991) and subsequent instruments. Commercial policy combines common rules — especially the NCM and the CET for third countries — with national implementing law.
frameworkPoints: NCM: shared eight-digit nomenclature, the statistical and customs basis for classifying goods across the bloc.
CET: reference schedule for imports originating outside Mercosur; Mercosur Common Market Council (CMC) decisions may change rates and benefits over time.
Paraguay applies national law on top of the NCM (current national tariff), periodically updated with HS amendments and bloc commitments.
CET reductions or adjustments (e.g. CMC-style decisions implemented by national decree) can cover thousands of lines — direct impact on landed cost.
Mercosur origin: specific documentation and rules to access intra-bloc benefits; essential in regional supply chains.
Paraguayan exports: sanitary, value-added and destination requirements must match each target market.
DivisionImportExport.mercosulTitle: Treaties, the bloc and day-to-day operations
DivisionImportExport.mercosulLead: Paraguay is a Mercosur State Party: that shapes intra-bloc preferences, macroeconomic coordination and common instruments — it does not replace professional advice on each customs clearance.
mercosulBullets: Agreements with third countries or groups (beyond the CET) complement the schedule: every shipment must match the treaty applicable to the good and the counterparty.
Free zones and special regimes exist in Paraguay’s customs space — eligibility depends on product, investment and specific regulation.
Disputes and bilateral consultations are part of the ecosystem; high-volume firms maintain continuous compliance and change monitoring.
Land logistics (waterways and roads) can position Paraguay as a redistribution hub when combined with correct classification and contractual lead times.

processSteps.title: Sourcing and supply
processSteps.body: Category and supplier selection with preliminary fiscal fit: technology, lifestyle, cosmetics and consumer goods — always with NCM and estimated customs cost.

processSteps.title: Logistics integration
processSteps.body: Cross-border flows and South American redistribution: timelines, insurance and documentation aligned to the mode (ocean, air, road).

processSteps.title: Go-to-market channels
processSteps.body: Local distribution, DTC, e-commerce and marketplaces per line strategy and marketplace rules for origin and compliance.

processSteps.title: Growth support
processSteps.body: Scale with group technology (Developer) when volume, integrations and data require platforms and automation.

faqs.q: Which import categories do you prioritise?
faqs.a: Electronics and technology, digital devices, cosmetics and dermocosmetics, and consumer & lifestyle with proven regional demand — always with NCM and tax mapping for the case.
Yes—exports, regional redistribution and logistics integration across South America are part of the model, respecting origin and destination requirements.

faqs.q: How does this division connect to the rest of the group?
faqs.a: It works tightly with Phoenix Global Developer (platforms, data, automation) and can align with corporate vehicles structured via Enterprise Solution when relevant.
Direct and marketplace channels exist; institutional enquiries on this site target businesses, distributors and partners.

faqs.q: Is the CET the same as the national tariff?
faqs.a: The CET is Mercosur’s common schedule for third countries; Paraguay publishes the national tariff that maps the NCM to rates and rules in force. For each NCM, use the table and instruments applicable on the clearance date.

faqs.q: How do you track tariff changes or bloc decisions?
faqs.a: Large operations need continuous monitoring of GMC/CMC decisions and national decrees. We work from documented assumptions and review when the instrument or classification changes.

DivisionImportExport.ctaLead: International trade with governance. Contact the team to align product, documentation and regional strategy.

DivisionDeveloper.metaTitle: Phoenix Global Developer | Phoenix Global Holding
DivisionDeveloper.metaDescription: AI, applications, web platforms and digital infrastructure for the group and corporate clients.
DivisionDeveloper.title: Phoenix Global Developer
DivisionDeveloper.subtitle: Product technology and digital infrastructure at international standard.
DivisionDeveloper.intro: We build capabilities in artificial intelligence, mobile and web applications, enterprise systems and API integration. Deliverables power internal group operations and client projects.

processSteps.title: Discovery and architecture
processSteps.body: Requirements, risks, integrations and data model alignment before production code.

processSteps.title: Iterative build
processSteps.body: Short cycles with continuous validation, testing and observability from early stages.

processSteps.title: Security and compliance
processSteps.body: Access controls, secret management and deployment practices suited to enterprise environments.

processSteps.title: Run and evolve
processSteps.body: Post-launch support, performance improvements and feature evolution driven by business metrics.

faqs.q: Do you build with generative AI?
faqs.a: Yes—integrating advanced models where they add real value: automation, analytics and assisted systems, with clear data governance.

faqs.q: Do you work only for the group?
faqs.a: Both—we strengthen the holding and deliver for external enterprises and digital initiatives that fit our profile.
We choose per engagement; modern web work often uses TypeScript, cloud-native patterns and solid CI/CD.

faqs.q: How does this connect to Import and Export?
faqs.a: Operational automation, digital commerce platforms and analytics are typical synergy areas.

matrixContent.detailHeadline: A development division built to ship, not to decorate
matrixContent.detailLead: Where others list generic bullets, Phoenix structures business lines, measurable deliverables and acceptance criteria before you sign.
matrixContent.superiorityTitle: Above the usual “custom dev” landing bar
superiorityItems: Ownership and repo from the contract — no end-of-project surprises
Phased checklists you can sign off — you know what you get each sprint
Negotiated quality floor — security, backups and observability
Real handover — documentation and session, not just a ZIP
matrixContent.comparisonHeading: Phoenix vs. a typical market pattern
patternBullets: One block “systems, apps, e-commerce” with no real scope
phoenixBullets: Product lines with scope and risks named
Proposal aligned to deliverables and data

serviceCards.title: Web platforms & systems
serviceCards.body: Backoffice, portals and business rules with permissions and audit.

serviceCards.title: Web experience & PWA
serviceCards.body: Fast, accessible UIs with solid technical SEO.

serviceCards.title: Commerce & payments
serviceCards.body: Stores and checkout with gateways aligned to your context.

serviceCards.title: APIs & integrations
serviceCards.body: Bridges between your stack and new tools.

serviceCards.title: Modernisation & maintenance
serviceCards.body: Legacy with a migration plan and controlled risk.

serviceCards.title: Product & data
serviceCards.body: Instrumentation and dashboards to decide with numbers.

matrixContent.deliverTitle: What we deliver on every project

deliverItems.text: Prioritised requirements, integrations and risks — living doc.
Git, branches, reviews — IP defined in contract.
Pipeline, secure env vars and staging / production.

matrixContent.qualityLead: Clear on what is in and out of scope.
matrixContent.stackLead: Stable tech and hireable talent; final stack comes from discovery.

stackRows.label: UI
stackRows.text: React, Next.js or HTML/CSS/JS when simplicity wins.

stackRows.label: Server
stackRows.text: Node.js, Python or .NET depending on integrations.

stackRows.label: Infra
stackRows.text: AWS, GCP, Vercel, Railway; IaC when justified.

matrixContent.ctaText: Short brief or call: range estimates, phased scope and explicit risks.

DivisionEnterprise.metaTitle: Phoenix Global Enterprise Solution | Phoenix Global Holding
DivisionEnterprise.metaDescription: Paraguay corporate structuring for foreign investors: EAS, RUC, operational activation and remote support.
DivisionEnterprise.title: Phoenix Global Enterprise Solution
DivisionEnterprise.subtitle: Corporate gateway to Paraguay—without relocating.
DivisionEnterprise.intro: We support international investors and entrepreneurs in incorporating and activating companies in Paraguay, with remote-first management and focus on legal and operational efficiency. This page is informational; each matter needs professional review.

processSteps.title: Diagnosis and plan
processSteps.body: Corporate purpose, shareholders, tax and operational needs before incorporation.

processSteps.title: EAS incorporation
processSteps.body: Bylaws, shareholder registry and corporate documentation under Paraguayan law.

processSteps.title: Tax and operational activation
processSteps.body: RUC (DNIT), electronic invoicing where applicable, customs registrations if required, and accounting baseline.

processSteps.title: Ongoing support
processSteps.body: Local representation, digital documentation, operational compliance and coordination with other group divisions when appropriate.

faqs.a: No—the model is designed for remote execution and local representation within applicable law.
It depends on complexity, documentation and agency timelines. We share realistic estimates after initial diagnosis.

faqs.q: Do you provide binding tax advice?
faqs.a: We publish general information; binding opinions come from licensed professionals for each case.

faqs.q: Does this integrate with Import and Export or Developer?
faqs.a: Yes—once the vehicle exists, other divisions can support trade operations, platforms and automation.
